title: Agoseris arizonica
intro: Agoseris arizonica, also known as Arizona agoseris, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The bioactive constituents of Agoseris arizonica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Agoseris arizonica is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Agoseris arizonica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is a member of the genus Agoseris, which consists of several species of dandelion-like plants native to North America. The family Asteraceae is commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family, and it includes over 23,000 known species. Agoseris arizonica is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, where it grows in dry, open areas. The exact classification of Agoseris arizonica may also be expressed in a more detailed binomial nomenclature system.
